items.title: Kering: Gucci and L'Oreal enter 50-year beauty licence, effective one year early
items.summary: Official Kering release confirming Gucci and L'Oreal signed a 50-year exclusive worldwide beauty licence, moved up to take effect July 2027 rather than mid-2028. Coty receives roughly $400 million for early redemption of its existing licence, paid across 2026-2027. CEO Luca de Meo said the deal lets Gucci and L'Oreal 'begin shaping the next chapter of Gucci Beauty a year earlier than planned.'

items.title: Gucci Beauty License Is Transferring to L'Oreal in Mid-2027
items.summary: WWD trade analysis of the accelerated L'Oreal-Gucci beauty deal, citing Citi's estimate that Gucci Beauty currently generates about 520 million euros with Barclays projecting a multi-billion-euro long-term opportunity under L'Oreal. Frames the licence as a structural bright spot distinct from the still-unresolved turnaround in Gucci's core leather goods and ready-to-wear business.

items.title: Gucci's Monte Carlo story continues
items.summary: Trade coverage of the second chapter of Gucci's 2026 Monte Carlo summer campaign, moving from coastline to hotel suites, villas and motorbike rides. Accessories lead the story: the Jackie in metallic mesh, a shimmering Dionysus, the Giglio in washed black GG denim and Flora-print versions, and the Gucci Venice in GG canvas, tied to the 60th anniversary of the Flora motif.

items.title: PurseForum thread: 2026 Gucci Flora
items.summary: Ongoing PurseForum discussion among collectors on the 2026 Flora-motif pieces under Demna, including the Flora-print nylon Jackie, Flora coated-canvas tote and Flora-motif silk twill GG Marmont. Members debate durability of coated canvas versus classic Gucci canvas and how the print nylon wears over time; Gucci remains the forum's fifth most active subforum after Hermes, Chanel, Louis Vuitton and Dior.

items.title: Forum thread: Gucci Monte Carlo Summer 2026 by Mark Seliger
items.summary: Fashion-forum thread reacting to the Monte Carlo campaign, with opinion split between praise ('finally looking like luxury,' 'Demna continues to impress') and criticism ('feels very cheap and dated,' 'where's the opulence?'). Included as background context for the July 13 second-chapter story since the thread remains the main community venue tracking the campaign's reception; flagged as older than the 72-hour target window.

items.title: Kering Q1 2026 slides: revenue stabilizes as jewelry offsets Gucci drag
items.summary: Analyst-facing recap noting Kering revenue stabilizing on group level while Gucci continues to weigh on results and jewelry (Boucheron/Pomellato/Qeelin) offsets the drag; used by European sell-side desks ahead of Kering's upcoming H1 print as a checkpoint on whether Gucci's decline is bottoming out.

items.title: Gucci Instagram reel: Monte Carlo campaign chapter one
items.summary: Official Gucci reel introducing the Monte Carlo campaign's first chapter, framed around 'motion, light, and the spirit of escape' in Monaco. Included as the brand's own-channel activation baseline; visible engagement counters on the scraped view were implausibly low relative to Gucci's typical 100K+ likes per post, so metrics below are left null rather than reported as unreliable numbers.
